conj.statement: All tiers agree that *pre-reflective bodily habits* (e.g., racialized vigilance, gendered posture) are the primary site of subject constitution—more foundational than linguistic representation. (Supported by: 2024 CDC racial bias in pain assessment; 2023 NIH embodied cognition studies.)How do AI-driven language systems (e.g., predictive policing, chatbot triage) *re-sediment* power in pre-reflective embodiment? Current 2026 data lacks longitudinal tracking of embodied habit change post-AI implementation. 2. **Transnational Lifeworlds**: For immigrant communities (e.g., Houston, Miami), how do *dual constitutional subjectivities* (U.S. law + homeland cultural logic) negotiate *constitutive power*? No existing policy framework addresses *transnational hermeneutic justice*. 3. **Existential Burnout**: In high-stress counties (e.g., border towns, post-industrial regions), does *over-embodiment of resistance* (e.g., constant “habitat audits”) risk *exhausting the lifeworld*, leading to apathetic subjectivity? Requires new metrics for *existential resilience*.
conj.subtopic: Power, Language, and the Constitution of the Subject
conj.county_variations: (No “one-size-fits-all”; all adaptations must preserve the integrity of the three layers)*
